summary: Implemented three frame constraints (nullity_identity, converse, forward_comp) in BimodalSemantics.build_frame_constraints() and created 8 dedicated unit tests. All 12 existing tests continue to pass.

artifacts.summary: Implementation summary covering all three phases: constraint methods added, frame constraints integrated, and test file created.
Added build_nullity_identity_constraint(), build_converse_constraint(), build_forward_comp_constraint() methods and integrated all three into build_frame_constraints().
New test file with 8 tests across 4 classes (TestNullityIdentity, TestConverse, TestForwardComp, TestConstraintInteractions).

completion_data.completion_summary: Added three frame constraint builder methods to BimodalSemantics (build_nullity_identity_constraint, build_converse_constraint, build_forward_comp_constraint) in semantic.py, integrated them into build_frame_constraints(), and created a dedicated test file with 8 passing unit tests. The implementation aligns with the Lean ProofChecker's TaskFrame axioms and Compositional typeclass from Frame.lean. All 12 pre-existing bimodal tests continue to pass.

memory_candidates.content: Z3 quantifier performance tip: when implementing compositionality constraints with 5 quantified variables (w, v, u, d1, d2), use z3.MultiPattern(premise1, premise2) in z3.ForAll(..., patterns=[...]) to guide instantiation only when both premises are in the ground term set. Without patterns, Z3 may generate exponential spurious instantiations. Duration validity guards (is_valid_duration) also reduce the instantiation scope significantly.

BimodalSemantics test fixture sizing: for unit tests that load the full frame_constraints list (including skolem_abundance_constraint which expands into nested ForAll with is_shifted_by), use N=2 (4 states) rather than N=3 (8 states). With N=3 and the full frame constraints, solver.check() can timeout. N=2 keeps solver time under 0.5s per test while still allowing 4 distinct states (0,1,2,3) for testing.
